title: Node HTTP Primitives, Connections, Protocols, Timeouts, and Shutdown · Type-System Trace: Close Idle Active Upgrades
summary: A Node server inherits defaults without understanding header, request, keep-alive, upgrade, idle-socket, HTTP/2, or graceful-close behavior. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts close idle active upgrades increment in Node HTTP server protocol and timeout budget. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate server.close, closeIdleConnections, closeAllConnections, upgraded sockets, active responses, and forced shutdown.
Distinguish HTTP/1.1, Node HTTP/2 compatibility behavior, ALPN, TLS, upgrades, CONNECT, and the explicit external boundary for HTTP/3.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Close Idle Active Upgrades · Theory Close Idle Active Upgrades
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 2978 in a browser application using cookie authentication across approved origins. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate protocol support with explicit header, request, and drain policy; keep rollback safe during a storage expansion; then fail the canary while the old revision drains and prove rollback remains safe. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
